Name: pac-bayes_latency_optimization
Title: Enforcing Latency Constraints in Machine Learning Inference through PAC-Bayes Frameworks
Short Hypothesis: Utilizing PAC-Bayes frameworks with spectral stability bounds can enforce strict latency constraints in ML models, leading to improved throughput and accuracy in real-time inference scenarios.
Related Work: While existing works like 'Dynamic Kernel Selection for Real-Time ML Inference' and 'OptimML' focus on adaptive strategies for latency and power optimization, they do not leverage the theoretical underpinnings of PAC-Bayes to enforce latency constraints. This proposal integrates PAC-Bayes bounds with spectral stability metrics to derive enforceable protocols for latency-critical tasks, positioning it uniquely in the literature.
Abstract: As machine learning models increasingly serve real-time applications, the necessity for strict latency management becomes paramount. This proposal explores the intersection of PAC-Bayes frameworks and spectral stability bounds to create enforceable protocols for latency-aware ML inference. By framing latency as a bounded constraint within the PAC-Bayes context, we aim to derive protocols that not only minimize hidden-state perturbation but also optimize throughput. The proposed framework will utilize a Jacobian-based layer-skipping mechanism to allow for real-time decision-making without incurring significant computational overhead. We will empirically validate this framework through controlled experiments that measure throughput and accuracy across various ML models in latency-critical environments. Expected outcomes include comprehensive benchmarks demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed methods in maintaining operational efficiency while adhering to strict latency requirements.
Experiments: Implement the PAC-Bayes framework with spectral stability bounds on common ML models (e.g., CNNs, Transformers) to test the latency protocols.
Conduct experiments to optimize the Jacobian-based layer-skipping mechanism under varying latency constraints, measuring throughput and accuracy trade-offs.
Evaluate performance across different hardware platforms to assess generalizability and robustness, with metrics including latency, accuracy, and memory usage.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the complexity of integrating PAC-Bayes constraints into existing frameworks and the challenge of generalizing findings across diverse hardware platforms. Limitations may arise from the necessity of empirical tuning in real-world applications, which could introduce variability in performance metrics.
